message:
feat(types): Add sanitization and scoring configuration schemas

Expands the type system with Zod schemas for modular configuration:

## New Schemas Added

- ExtraPatternSchema: Validates custom sanitization patterns with
  pattern (RegExp) and replacement (string) fields
- SanitizationConfigSchema: Configuration for security sanitization
  with enabled flag and extraPatterns array, defaults enabled=true
- ScoringConfigSectionSchema: Scoring algorithm parameters including:
  * decayHalfLifeDays (default: 90)
  * harmfulMultiplier (default: 4)
  * minFeedbackForActive (default: 3)
  * minHelpfulForProven (default: 10)
  * maxHarmfulRatioForProven (default: 0.1)

## ConfigSchema Updates

- Added nested scoring configuration object
- Made scoring section optional at top level to support partial overrides
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing config files

These schemas enable type-safe configuration loading and validation
throughout the ACE pipeline (Generator → Reflector → Validator → Curator).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.8","title":"Playbook YAML Read/Write: src/playbook.ts","description":"# Feature: Playbook YAML Read/Write (src/playbook.ts)

## Purpose
Handle all playbook operations: loading, saving, creating new playbooks,
and cascading global + repo-level playbooks.

## Playbook File Format
```yaml
schema_version: 2
name: "project-playbook"
description: "Auto-generated from cass-memory reflections"

metadata:
  createdAt: "2025-12-01T00:00:00Z"
  lastReflection: "2025-12-07T10:00:00Z"
  totalReflections: 42
  totalSessionsProcessed: 156

deprecatedPatterns:
  - pattern: "AuthHandler"
    deprecatedAt: "2025-10-01"
    reason: "Moved to AuthService v2"
    replacement: "Use AuthService from @/lib/auth"

bullets:
  - id: "b-1a2b3c4d-x7y8"
    category: "testing"
    scope: "global"
    kind: "stack_pattern"
    state: "proven"
    content: |
      For React hooks, test returned values and effects separately
      using renderHook from @testing-library/react-hooks
    searchPointer: "cass search 'react hook testing renderHook'"
    feedbackEvents:
      - type: "helpful"
        timestamp: "2025-12-05T09:00:00Z"
        sessionPath: "~/.claude/sessions/abc.jsonl"
    helpfulCount: 8
    harmfulCount: 1
    tags: ["react", "testing", "hooks"]
    sourceSessions:
      - "~/.claude/projects/app/session-001.jsonl:142"
    sourceAgents: ["claude", "cursor"]
    createdAt: "2025-11-15T14:30:00Z"
    updatedAt: "2025-12-05T09:00:00Z"
```

## Functions to Implement
```typescript
// Load single playbook from path
async function loadPlaybook(path: string): Promise<Playbook>;

// Load merged playbook (global + repo)
async function loadMergedPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>;

// Save playbook to path
async function savePlaybook(playbook: Playbook, path: string): Promise<void>;

// Create empty playbook
function createEmptyPlaybook(name?: string): Playbook;

// Merge two playbooks (for cascading)
function mergePlaybooks(global: Playbook, repo: Playbook | null): Playbook;

// Find bullet by ID
function findBullet(playbook: Playbook, id: string): PlaybookBullet | undefined;

// Add bullet to playbook (with dedup check)
function addBullet(playbook: Playbook, bullet: NewBulletData): PlaybookBullet;

// Update bullet
function updateBullet(playbook: Playbook, id: string, updates: Partial<PlaybookBullet>): boolean;
```

## Cascading Logic ("Memory as Code")
When in a git repo with `.cass/playbook.yaml`:
1. Load global playbook from ~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml
2. Load repo playbook from .cass/playbook.yaml
3. Merge with repo taking precedence for same-ID bullets
4. Filter out toxic bullets from both levels
5. Return merged playbook

This enables "team brain" - new developers inherit project rules automatically.

## Toxic Bullet Filtering
```typescript
const globalToxic = await loadToxicLog("~/.cass-memory/toxic_bullets.log");
const repoToxic = await loadToxicLog("./.cass/toxic.log");
merged.bullets = merged.bullets.filter(b =>
  !isSemanticallyToxic(b.content, [...globalToxic, ...repoToxic])
);
```","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:18:49.646748-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.195092-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.8","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:18:49.647737-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.8","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.059588-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.8","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.142259-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.8","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.210115-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.9","title":"LLM Provider Abstraction: src/llm.ts","description":"# Feature: LLM Provider Abstraction (src/llm.ts)

## Purpose
Abstract LLM interactions using Vercel AI SDK, supporting multiple providers
(OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) with a unified interface.

## Why Vercel AI SDK?
1. Provider-agnostic: Same code works with any provider
2. Structured output: generateObject with Zod schemas
3. Streaming: Built-in support (useful for future MCP server)
4. Type safety: Full TypeScript support

## Provider Setup
```typescript
import { createOpenAI } from "@ai-sdk/openai";
import { createAnthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
import { createGoogleGenerativeAI } from "@ai-sdk/google";
import { generateObject, generateText } from "ai";

function getModel(config: Config) {
  const apiKey = getApiKeyFromEnv(config.provider);
  
  switch (config.provider) {
    case "openai": return createOpenAI({ apiKey })(config.model);
    case "anthropic": return createAnthropic({ apiKey })(config.model);
    case "google": return createGoogleGenerativeAI({ apiKey })(config.model);
  }
}
```

## Environment Variables
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` for OpenAI
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for Anthropic
- `GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY` for Google

## Functions to Implement

### Structured Output (for reflection pipeline)
```typescript
async function extractDiary<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  sessionContent: string,
  metadata: SessionMetadata,
  config: Config
): Promise<T>;

async function runReflector<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  diary: DiaryEntry,
  existingBullets: string,
  cassHistory: string,
  iteration: number,
  config: Config
): Promise<T>;

async function runValidator<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  proposedRule: string,
  evidence: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<T>;
```

### Text Generation (for context)
```typescript
async function generateContext(
  task: string,
  bullets: string,
  history: string,
  deprecatedPatterns: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<string>;

async function generateSearchQueries(
  task: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<string[]>;
```

## Prompt Templates
Defined in PROMPTS constant:
- `PROMPTS.diary` - Extract structured diary from session
- `PROMPTS.reflector` - Generate delta proposals
- `PROMPTS.validator` - Validate rules against evidence
- `PROMPTS.context` - Generate pre-task briefing
- `PROMPTS.audit` - Check session for rule violations

## Error Handling
- Rate limit handling with exponential backoff
- Token limit checking before sending
- Graceful degradation if API unavailable","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:19:49.140355-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:19:49.140355-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.9","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:19:49.141377-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.9","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.286016-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.9","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:20:12.369566-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5iw","title":"[Utils] ensureDir - Recursive directory creation","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:46.30019-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:46.30019-05:00","comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-5iw","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function ensureDir(dir: string): Promise<void>
```

## Implementation Details
- Expand path using expandPath() first
- Use fs.promises.mkdir(expandedPath, { recursive: true })
- Handle EEXIST error gracefully (directory already exists)
- Throw other errors (permission denied, invalid path, etc.)

## Why This Matters
Used before writing any file to ensure parent directories exist. Prevents ENOENT errors when writing to nested paths like "~/.cass-memory/sessions/2024/session.md". The recursive option creates all parent directories in one call.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Directory already exists - should succeed silently
- Parent directory doesn't exist - create entire path
- Permission denied - should throw with helpful message
- Invalid path characters - should throw
- Empty string - should throw or handle gracefully
- Path is a file, not directory - mkdir will fail, let it throw","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-5zf","title":"[Security] Sanitization audit logging","description":"Implement sanitization audit logging to track when secrets are redacted.

Requirements:
1. When sanitization replaces something, log count of replacements
2. NEVER log actual secrets or matched text
3. Log format: "[sanitize] Replaced N potential secrets in <context>"
4. Make logging optional via config flag

Log levels:
- INFO: Summary of replacements ("Replaced 3 potential secrets")
- DEBUG: Which pattern types matched ("Replaced: 2 AWS keys, 1 Bearer token")
- NEVER: Actual secret values or surrounding context

Example logs:
```
[sanitize] Replaced 3 potential secrets in session-abc123
[sanitize] Patterns matched: AWS_ACCESS_KEY(1), BEARER_TOKEN(2)
[sanitize] No secrets detected in session-xyz789
```

Configuration:
```json
{
  "sanitization": {
    "enabled": true,
    "auditLog": true,  // Enable audit logging
    "auditLevel": "info"  // "info" or "debug"
  }
}
```

Purpose:
- Understand what's being caught
- Verify sanitization is working
- Debug pattern effectiveness
- Compliance/security auditing

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:53.427417-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:57.882946-05:00"}
@@ -131,7 +131,7 @@
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-be2","title":"[Decay] analyzeScoreDistribution - Playbook health metrics","description":"Analyze the distribution of effective scores across all bullets in a playbook. Provides health metrics showing how many rules are excellent, good, neutral, or at-risk. Used by stats command to show playbook quality.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Analyze score distribution across all bullets.
 * Provides playbook health metrics and identifies problematic rules.
 */
function analyzeScoreDistribution(bullets: PlaybookBullet[]): {
  excellent: number;      // score > 10
  good: number;          // score 5-10
  neutral: number;       // score 0-5
  atRisk: number;        // score < 0
  distribution: number[]; // histogram buckets
  avgScore: number;
  medianScore: number;
  totalBullets: number;
} {
  const scores = bullets.map(b => getEffectiveScore(b));
  
  // Category counts
  const excellent = scores.filter(s => s > 10).length;
  const good = scores.filter(s => s > 5 && s <= 10).length;
  const neutral = scores.filter(s => s >= 0 && s <= 5).length;
  const atRisk = scores.filter(s => s < 0).length;

  // Histogram: buckets from -5 to 15 in steps of 2
  const buckets = Array(11).fill(0);
  scores.forEach(score => {
    const bucketIndex = Math.min(10, Math.max(0, Math.floor((score + 5) / 2)));
    buckets[bucketIndex]++;
  });

  // Statistics
  const avgScore = scores.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s, 0) / scores.length;
  const sorted = [...scores].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const medianScore = scores.length % 2 === 0
    ? (sorted[scores.length / 2 - 1] + sorted[scores.length / 2]) / 2
    : sorted[Math.floor(scores.length / 2)];

  return {
    excellent,
    good,
    neutral,
    atRisk,
    distribution: buckets,
    avgScore: Math.round(avgScore * 100) / 100,
    medianScore: Math.round(medianScore * 100) / 100,
    totalBullets: bullets.length
  };
}
```

SCORE CATEGORIES:

Excellent (score > 10):
- Highly proven rules with strong positive signal
- Typically proven maturity with 8+ helpful, 0 harmful
- Core playbook rules that define best practices

Good (score 5-10):
- Solid established or proven rules
- Consistent positive feedback
- Reliable guidance with few issues

Neutral (score 0-5):
- New candidates or low-feedback rules
- Neither strongly positive nor negative
- May need more time to prove value

At Risk (score < 0):
- Rules with harmful marks outweighing helpful
- Candidates for demotion or deprecation
- Need review - may be toxic or obsolete

HISTOGRAM BUCKETS:
```
Bucket 0: score < -5
Bucket 1: -5 to -3
Bucket 2: -3 to -1
Bucket 3: -1 to 1
Bucket 4: 1 to 3
Bucket 5: 3 to 5
Bucket 6: 5 to 7
Bucket 7: 7 to 9
Bucket 8: 9 to 11
Bucket 9: 11 to 13
Bucket 10: score > 13
```

USAGE IN STATS COMMAND:
```typescript
const analysis = analyzeScoreDistribution(playbook.bullets);

console.log('📊 Playbook Health Report');
console.log(`Total rules: ${analysis.totalBullets}`);
console.log(`Excellent (>10): ${analysis.excellent} (${pct(analysis.excellent)}%)`);
console.log(`Good (5-10): ${analysis.good} (${pct(analysis.good)}%)`);
console.log(`Neutral (0-5): ${analysis.neutral} (${pct(analysis.neutral)}%)`);
console.log(`At Risk (<0): ${analysis.atRisk} (${pct(analysis.atRisk)}%)`);
console.log(`Average score: ${analysis.avgScore}`);
console.log(`Median score: ${analysis.medianScore}`);

// ASCII histogram
console.log('\nScore Distribution:');
analysis.distribution.forEach((count, i) => {
  const scoreRange = `${i*2-5} to ${i*2-3}`;
  const bar = '█'.repeat(Math.floor(count / 2));
  console.log(`${scoreRange.padEnd(10)} ${bar} (${count})`);
});
```

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:
```
📊 Playbook Health Report
Total rules: 42
Excellent (>10): 8 (19%)
Good (5-10): 15 (36%)
Neutral (0-5): 16 (38%)
At Risk (<0): 3 (7%)
Average score: 6.23
Median score: 5.50

Score Distribution:
-5 to -3   ██ (2)
-3 to -1   █ (1)
-1 to 1    ████ (4)
1 to 3     ████████ (8)
3 to 5     ██████ (6)
5 to 7     ██████████ (10)
7 to 9     ████ (4)
9 to 11    ████ (4)
11 to 13   ██ (2)
>13        █ (1)
```

HEALTH INDICATORS:

Healthy Playbook:
- 60%+ in good/excellent
- <10% at risk
- Median score > 5
- Distribution skews right (positive)

Warning Signs:
- 20%+ at risk
- Median score < 2
- Many rules in -3 to 1 range
- Few or no excellent rules

Critical Issues:
- 50%+ at risk
- Average score < 0
- Distribution skews left (negative)
- Indicates playbook decay or mismatch with codebase

DEPENDENCIES:
- getEffectiveScore()
- PlaybookBullet array

USED BY:
- stats command
- Playbook health dashboard (future)
- Automated alerts (future)
- Quality metrics API (future)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:59.865436-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:43.927482-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bjz","title":"[LLM] llmWithRetry - Exponential backoff retry wrapper","description":"Function signature:
async function llmWithRetry<T>(
  operation: () => Promise<T>,
  operationName: string
): Promise<T>

Configuration:
- maxRetries: 3
- baseDelayMs: 1000
- maxDelayMs: 30000
- retryableErrors: ['rate_limit_exceeded', 'server_error', 'timeout', 'overloaded']

Logic:
1. Try operation
2. If error thrown:
   a. Check if error type is retryable
   b. If yes and retries < maxRetries:
      - Calculate delay: min(baseDelayMs * 2^attempt, maxDelayMs)
      - Log retry attempt with delay
      - Wait
      - Retry operation
   c. If not retryable or max retries exceeded, throw error
3. Return result on success

Logging example:
'LLM operation extractDiary failed (rate_limit_exceeded). Retrying in 2000ms... (attempt 2/3)'

Use this wrapper around all LLM operations for resilience against transient failures.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:09.625399-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:08.984546-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bm7","title":"[Reflector] getIterationNote - Context for subsequent iterations","description":"function getIterationNote(iteration: number): string

- iteration 0: empty string
- iteration 1+: "This is iteration {N+1}. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."

PURPOSE:
Helps LLM find additional insights on subsequent passes

EXAMPLES:
- iteration 0: "" (first pass is broad)
- iteration 1: "This is iteration 2. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."
- iteration 2: "This is iteration 3. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences."

WHY THIS WORKS:
- Iteration 0: LLM finds obvious patterns
- Iteration 1+: Prompt guides LLM to look deeper
- Specific guidance: "edge cases, anti-patterns, subtle preferences"

IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
if (iteration === 0) return ''
return `This is iteration ${iteration + 1}. Focus on insights you may have missed in previous passes. Consider edge cases, anti-patterns, and subtle preferences.`
```

Used in buildReflectorPrompt()","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.16202-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:00.18406-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bq3a","title":"[Semantic] scoreBulletsEnhanced - Keyword + semantic scoring","description":"Enhanced bullet scoring combining keyword matching with semantic similarity.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function scoreBulletsEnhanced(
  bullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  task: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<ScoredBullet[]>
```

Steps:
1. Keyword scoring (fast, existing)
2. Get task embedding
3. For each bullet:
   - Load or compute bullet embedding
   - Calculate cosine similarity
4. Combined score: 40% keyword + 60% semantic
5. Final: combined × effective score × maturity multiplier
6. Sort and return

Why hybrid scoring:
- Keywords catch exact matches
- Semantics catch conceptual matches
- Combined provides best relevance

Example:
Task: "fix authentication bug"
- Keyword match: "auth" → score 2
- Semantic match: "login session handling" → similarity 0.82
- Combined: (2 * 0.4) + (8.2 * 0.6) = 5.72

Performance:
- 100 bullets: ~300ms (including embeddings)
- With cache: ~50ms (embeddings pre-computed)

Config:
- semanticSearchEnabled: true/false
- semanticWeight: 0.6 (adjustable)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:30.813559-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:57.053823-05:00","comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-bq3a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Enhanced bullet scoring combining keyword matching with semantic similarity.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function scoreBulletsEnhanced(
  bullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  task: string,
  config: Config
): Promise<ScoredBullet[]>
```

Steps:
1. Keyword scoring (fast, existing)
2. Get task embedding
3. For each bullet:
   - Load or compute bullet embedding
   - Calculate cosine similarity
4. Combined score: 40% keyword + 60% semantic
5. Final: combined × effective score × maturity multiplier
6. Sort and return

Why hybrid scoring:
- Keywords catch exact matches
- Semantics catch conceptual matches
- Combined provides best relevance

Example:
Task: "fix authentication bug"
- Keyword match: "auth" → score 2
- Semantic match: "login session handling" → similarity 0.82
- Combined: (2 * 0.4) + (8.2 * 0.6) = 5.72

Performance:
- 100 bullets: ~300ms (including embeddings)
- With cache: ~50ms (embeddings pre-computed)

Config:
- semanticSearchEnabled: true/false
- semanticWeight: 0.6 (adjustable)","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-by51","title":"[CLI] why command - Show bullet origin evidence","description":"Implements the 'cass-memory why' command to show the origin evidence for a specific bullet.

COMMAND SYNTAX:
```bash
cass-memory why <bullet-id> [options]

Arguments:
  bullet-id      The bullet ID to explain

Options:
  --verbose      Show full session transcripts
  --json         Output as JSON
```

PURPOSE:
Answers "Why was this rule learned?" by showing the original reasoning, source sessions, evidence quotes, and diary entries that contributed to creating this bullet.

OUTPUT FORMAT (Terminal):
```
WHY WAS THIS RULE LEARNED?
===========================

Bullet: abc-123
Content: "Use React.memo for expensive renders"
Category: Performance | Maturity: proven
Created: 2024-11-15 (83 days ago)

ORIGINAL REASONING:
  Agent noticed repeated performance issues with re-rendering in
  session 45a3b. Pattern of wrapping expensive components in React.memo
  led to 3x speedup. Validated across 2 subsequent sessions.

SOURCE SESSIONS (3):
  1. Session 45a3b (2024-11-15): Performance optimization
     "Wrapped DataTable in React.memo, renders dropped from 120ms to 40ms"
     
  2. Session 6f2c9 (2024-11-18): Similar pattern applied
     "Used same memo pattern for ChartWidget, similar improvement"
     
  3. Session 8d1e4 (2024-11-22): Validation
     "React.memo approach confirmed effective for list components"

KEY EVIDENCE QUOTES:
  • "massive performance improvement after memoization"
  • "prevents unnecessary re-renders when props unchanged"
  • "measured 3x speedup on heavy components"

DIARY ENTRIES:
  2024-11-15: "Learned to wrap expensive renders in React.memo"
  2024-11-18: "React.memo pattern continues to be effective"

FEEDBACK HISTORY (8 helpful, 0 harmful):
  2024-11-20: Marked helpful (session 7a4d2)
  2024-11-25: Marked helpful (session 9c3e1)
  2024-12-01: Marked helpful (session 2f5b8)
  ... (5 more)

CURRENT STATUS:
  Score: 12.4 (proven)
  Last applied: 2 days ago
  Effectiveness: Very high
```

IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. Load bullet by ID from playbook
2. Retrieve original reasoning/justification
3. Find source sessions (sessions that contributed to this bullet)
4. Extract evidence quotes from source sessions
5. Find related diary entries by date range
6. Show feedback history
7. Display current status

DATA SOURCES:

Bullet Metadata:
```typescript
interface Bullet {
  id: string;
  content: string;
  reasoning?: string;  // Original LLM reasoning
  sourceSessions?: string[];  // Session IDs that led to this
  evidence?: string[];  // Key quotes
  createdAt: string;
  feedback: FeedbackEvent[];
}
```

Session Lookup:
- Query cass for sessions by ID
- Extract relevant conversation snippets
- Show context around evidence

Diary Lookup:
- Find entries within ±7 days of creation
- Match by keywords from bullet content
- Show contributing diary insights

VERBOSE MODE:
With --verbose flag:
- Show full session transcripts
- Include all evidence (not just key quotes)
- Show complete diary entries
- Display LLM's full reasoning chain

JSON OUTPUT:
```json
{
  "bullet": {
    "id": "abc-123",
    "content": "Use React.memo for expensive renders",
    "score": 12.4,
    "maturity": "proven"
  },
  "reasoning": "Agent noticed repeated performance...",
  "sourceSessions": [
    {
      "id": "45a3b",
      "date": "2024-11-15",
      "summary": "Performance optimization",
      "evidenceQuote": "Wrapped DataTable in React.memo..."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "massive performance improvement after memoization",
    ...
  ],
  "diaryEntries": [
    {
      "date": "2024-11-15",
      "content": "Learned to wrap expensive renders..."
    }
  ],
  "feedbackHistory": [...]
}
```

USE CASES:
- Understanding why a rule was created
- Validating bullet relevance
- Debugging incorrect rules
- Auditing learning process
- Building trust in the system

EDGE CASES:
- Bullet has no source sessions → show reasoning only
- Source sessions deleted/pruned → show warning
- No evidence available → show creation metadata
- Very old bullet → warn if cass index pruned

DEPENDENCIES:
- Playbook loader
- Cass session retrieval
- Diary loader
- Text highlighting/formatting
- Date utilities

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Complete provenance chain shown
- Evidence is compelling and relevant
- Easy to understand why rule was created
- Helps validate or question bullet accuracy
- Performance <2 seconds even with verbose mode","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:57:48.117272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:57:48.117272-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-p8p","title":"[Utils] normalizeLineEndings - Cross-platform line ending handling","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:27.161664-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:27.161664-05:00","comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-p8p","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function normalizeLineEndings(content: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Replace \r\n (Windows CRLF) with \n: content.replace(/\r\n/g, '\n')
- Replace \r (old Mac CR) with \n: result.replace(/\r/g, '\n')
- Return normalized content with Unix-style line endings (LF only)
- Order matters: replace CRLF before CR to avoid double replacement

## Why This Matters
Critical for cross-platform compatibility when processing session files. Git on Windows might checkout with CRLF, but processing expects LF. Ensures consistent behavior across macOS, Linux, and Windows.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- No line endings (single line) - return unchanged
- Mixed line endings in same file - all normalized to LF
- Empty string - return empty
- Only line endings (no content) - return normalized line endings
- Binary content - may corrupt, but session files are text only","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-pbwy","title":"[Semantic] findSimilarBulletsSemantic - Embedding-based search","description":"Find semantically similar bullets using embeddings.

Implementation:
```typescript
async function findSimilarBulletsSemantic(
  query: string,
  bullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  topK: number = 5
): Promise<{ bullet: PlaybookBullet; similarity: number }[]>
```

Process:
1. Embed query text
2. For each bullet:
   - Get or compute embedding
   - Calculate cosine similarity
3. Sort by similarity (descending)
4. Return top K results

Use cases:
- "similar" command: Find bullets like a query
- Duplicate detection: Find bullets too similar
- Related content: Suggest related rules

Example usage:

const similar = await findSimilarBulletsSemantic(

  playbook.bullets,

);

//   { bullet: {...}, similarity: 0.87 },  // "log failed login attempts"
//   { bullet: {...}, similarity: 0.81 },  // "return 401 for auth failures"
//   ...
// ]
```

Performance:
- 100 bullets: ~60ms
- 1000 bullets: ~600ms
- Could optimize with vector index (future)

## Memory System: cass-memory

Before starting complex tasks, retrieve relevant context:

```

- When harmful: `cass-memory mark <id> --harmful`

### Memory Protocol

2. REFERENCE: Cite bullet IDs when following rules
3. FEEDBACK: Mark helpful/harmful during work
4. REFLECT: Diary auto-generated after sessions

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-pqbp","title":"[CLI] similar command - Semantic bullet search","description":"Implements the 'cass-memory similar' command for semantic bullet search using embeddings.

COMMAND SYNTAX:
```bash
cass-memory similar "<query>" [options]

Arguments:
  query          Text query to find similar bullets

Options:
  --limit N      Number of results (default: 5)
  --threshold T  Minimum similarity score 0-1 (default: 0.7)
  --scope SCOPE  Filter by scope (global, workspace, all)
  --json         Output as JSON
```

PURPOSE:
Uses embeddings to find semantically similar bullets based on a natural language query. Useful for discovering related rules, finding duplicates, and exploring the playbook.

OUTPUT FORMAT (Terminal):
```
SIMILAR BULLETS TO: "handling authentication errors"
====================================================

Found 5 semantically similar bullets:

1. [Similarity: 0.92] Retry auth requests with exponential backoff
   ID: abc-123 | Category: API | Score: 8.4
   "When authentication fails with 401, retry with exponential..."

2. [Similarity: 0.87] Handle token expiry gracefully
   ID: def-456 | Category: Security | Score: 6.2
   "Check token expiry before API calls and refresh proactively..."

3. [Similarity: 0.81] Log auth failures for security monitoring
   ID: ghi-789 | Category: Security | Score: 5.1
   "Always log authentication failures with context for audit..."

4. [Similarity: 0.76] Show user-friendly error on auth failure
   ID: jkl-012 | Category: UX | Score: 4.3
   "Display clear message when auth fails, avoid technical jargon..."

5. [Similarity: 0.72] Use OAuth refresh tokens correctly
   ID: mno-345 | Category: Security | Score: 7.8
   "Store refresh tokens securely and use to obtain new access..."

TIP: Use 'cass-memory show <id>' to see full details
```

IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. Parse query and options
2. Generate embedding for query text
3. Load playbook with embeddings
4. Compute cosine similarity between query and all bullets
5. Filter by threshold and scope
6. Sort by similarity descending
7. Take top N results
8. Display with formatting

EMBEDDING SYSTEM:

Query Embedding:
```typescript
const queryEmbedding = await generateEmbedding(query);
```

Similarity Calculation:
```typescript
function cosineSimilarity(vec1: number[], vec2: number[]): number {
  const dotProduct = vec1.reduce((sum, val, i) => sum + val * vec2[i], 0);
  const mag1 = Math.sqrt(vec1.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val * val, 0));
  const mag2 = Math.sqrt(vec2.reduce((sum, val) => sum + val * val, 0));
  return dotProduct / (mag1 * mag2);
}
```

Bullet Matching:
```typescript
const matches = bullets.map(bullet => ({
  bullet,
  similarity: cosineSimilarity(queryEmbedding, bullet.embedding)
}))
.filter(m => m.similarity >= threshold)
.sort((a, b) => b.similarity - a.similarity)
.slice(0, limit);
```

DISPLAYED INFORMATION:
- Similarity score (0-1 scale)
- Bullet ID
- Category
- Current effectiveness score
- Content preview (truncated)

FILTERS:
- --threshold: minimum similarity (default 0.7)
- --limit: max results (default 5)
- --scope: global, workspace, or all

JSON OUTPUT:
```json
{
  "query": "handling authentication errors",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "abc-123",
      "similarity": 0.92,
      "content": "Retry auth requests with exponential backoff",
      "category": "API",
      "score": 8.4,
      "preview": "When authentication fails with 401..."
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

USE CASES:
- Find related rules before creating new one
- Discover duplicate or overlapping bullets
- Explore playbook by concept
- Find rules by natural language description
- Identify merge candidates

EDGE CASES:
- No embeddings available → error with setup instructions
- No results above threshold → show message
- Query too short → suggest more specific query
- Very high similarity (>0.95) → flag as potential duplicate

PERFORMANCE:
- Cache embeddings in memory
- Use FAISS or similar for large playbooks (1000+ bullets)
- Lazy load embeddings on first use
- Target <1 second for 500 bullets

FALLBACK:
If embeddings not available:
- Suggest enabling embeddings
- Offer keyword-based search as alternative
- Explain benefits of semantic search

DEPENDENCIES:
- Embedding generation (OpenAI/local)
- Playbook with embeddings
- Vector similarity utilities
- Terminal formatting

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Semantically relevant results
- Fast response time (<1s typical)
- Similarity scores make sense
- Helps discover related content
- Clear when duplicates found","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:58:16.13367-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:55.723841-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ps2","title":"[Playbook] findMergeCandidates - Group similar bullets for merging","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function findMergeCandidates(
  bullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  minSimilarity: number = 0.7
): PlaybookBullet[][]
```

## Purpose
Group similar bullets that could be merged. Used by stats command to suggest consolidation opportunities.

## Algorithm
1. Filter to active bullets only (exclude deprecated)
2. Group bullets by category first
3. Within each category:
   - Calculate pairwise similarity for all bullets
   - Cluster bullets with similarity >= minSimilarity
   - Form groups of 2+ similar bullets
4. Return array of groups

## Output Format
```typescript
[
  [bullet1, bullet2, bullet3],  // Group 1: 3 similar bullets
  [bullet4, bullet5],           // Group 2: 2 similar bullets
]
```

## Clustering Strategy
Options:
1. **Simple pairwise**: If A~B and B~C, group {A,B,C}
2. **Transitive closure**: Build similarity graph, find connected components
3. **Hierarchical**: Use complete-linkage clustering

Recommended: Transitive closure for best results

## Category Grouping
Only compare bullets in same category:
- Testing bullets with Testing bullets
- Security bullets with Security bullets
- Prevents false positives from shared terms

## Usage in Stats Command
```typescript
const groups = findMergeCandidates(playbook.bullets, 0.7);
console.log(`Found ${groups.length} merge opportunities:`);
groups.forEach((group, i) => {
  console.log(`\nGroup ${i + 1}:`);
  group.forEach(b => console.log(`  - ${b.content}`));
});
```

## Similarity Threshold
- **0.7**: Good default, catches related rules
- **0.8**: Stricter, only very similar
- **0.6**: Looser, may need manual review

## Implementation Notes
- Skip deprecated bullets entirely
- Group by category first (performance)
- Return only groups with 2+ bullets
- Sort groups by size (largest first)

## Dependencies
- jaccardSimilarity() utility
- Category field on bullets

## Related Functions
- findSimilarBullet() - finds single best match
- isDuplicateBullet() - boolean duplicate check
- deprecateBullet() - used when merging","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:08.411547-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:53.733991-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-pub","title":"[Types] ConfigSchema - Application Configuration Zod Schema","description":"## Overview
Implement comprehensive Zod schema for application-wide configuration with validation, defaults, and type safety.

## Required Fields

### Core Settings
- schema_version: number (default: 1) - Config file version for migrations
- provider: enum 'openai' | 'anthropic' | 'google' (default: 'anthropic') - LLM provider selection
- model: string (default: 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514') - Model identifier
- cassPath: string (default: 'cass') - Path to cass executable
- playbookPath: string (default: '~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml') - Main playbook location
- diaryDir: string (default: '~/.cass-memory/diary') - Working memory directory

### Reflector Configuration
- maxReflectorIterations: number, range 1-10 (default: 3) - Max iterations for reflector loop
- autoReflect: boolean (default: false) - Automatic reflection after sessions

### Deduplication & Pruning
- dedupSimilarityThreshold: number, range 0-1 (default: 0.85) - Cosine similarity threshold for merging bullets
- pruneHarmfulThreshold: number, range 1-10 (default: 3) - Harmful marks before auto-retirement
- defaultDecayHalfLife: number (default: 90) - Days for confidence decay half-life

### Context Limits
- maxBulletsInContext: number, range 5-200 (default: 50) - Max bullets in pre-task hydration
- maxHistoryInContext: number, range 3-50 (default: 10) - Max history snippets per context

### Lookback Windows
- sessionLookbackDays: number, range 1-365 (default: 7) - Days for session search in context
- validationLookbackDays: number, range 30-365 (default: 90) - Days for scientific validation

### Feature Flags
- validationEnabled: boolean (default: true) - Enable scientific validation before adding rules
- enrichWithCrossAgent: boolean (default: true) - Include multi-agent history in context
- verbose: boolean (default: false) - Detailed logging output
- jsonOutput: boolean (default: false) - Machine-readable JSON output

### Sanitization (Nested Object)
- sanitization.enabled: boolean (default: true) - Enable secret sanitization
- sanitization.extraPatterns: string[] (default: []) - Additional regex patterns for sanitization

## Implementation Details
- Use Zod's .default() for all optional fields
- Implement .refine() for cross-field validation (e.g., maxBulletsInContext > maxHistoryInContext)
- Add .transform() to expand ~ in paths
- Include helpful error messages via .describe()
- Export both schema and inferred TypeScript type

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 20+ config fields with correct types and ranges
- Default values match specification
- Range validation for numeric fields
- Enum validation for provider/model
- Path expansion for ~ in file paths
- Type-safe TypeScript interface exported
- Comprehensive JSDoc comments","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:54.88196-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:34.686171-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-pwp","title":"[Generator] formatLastHelpful - Human-readable feedback time","description":"Format bullet feedback timestamps as human-readable relative time.

SIGNATURE:
function formatLastHelpful(bullet: PlaybookBullet): string

PURPOSE:
Convert the most recent helpful event timestamp into a user-friendly relative time string for display in context output. Helps users understand recency of validation.

INPUT:
PlaybookBullet with events: Array<{
  type: 'helpful' | 'unhelpful',
  timestamp: Date,
  sessionPath?: string,
  notes?: string
}>

LOGIC:
1. Find most recent event where type === 'helpful'
2. If no helpful events exist, return 'Never'
3. Calculate time difference from now
4. Format based on magnitude:
   - < 1 hour: '{minutes} minutes ago'
   - < 24 hours: '{hours} hours ago'
   - < 7 days: '{days} days ago'
   - < 30 days: '{weeks} weeks ago'
   - < 365 days: '{months} months ago'
   - >= 365 days: '{years} years ago'

EXAMPLES:
- Last helpful 45 minutes ago → '45 minutes ago'
- Last helpful 3 hours ago → '3 hours ago'
- Last helpful 2 days ago → '2 days ago'
- Last helpful 3 weeks ago → '3 weeks ago'
- Last helpful 5 months ago → '5 months ago'
- Last helpful 2 years ago → '2 years ago'
- No helpful events → 'Never'

ROUNDING:
- Always round down (floor)
- 1.9 days → '1 day ago' (not 2)
- This is conventional for relative time

SPECIAL CASES:
- 'just now' for < 1 minute (optional)
- 'today' for same calendar day (optional)
- 'yesterday' for previous calendar day (optional)

FORMATTING OPTIONS:
Basic: '{n} {unit}s ago'
Friendly: 'last week', 'yesterday', 'just now'

IMPLEMENTATION:
Use date-fns or similar library for robust relative time:
import { formatDistanceToNow } from 'date-fns';
return formatDistanceToNow(lastHelpfulEvent.timestamp, { addSuffix: true });

OUTPUT USAGE:
Appears in context markdown:
  - Score: 4.2 | Last helpful: 2 days ago

Helps user assess:
- Recency of validation (recent = high confidence)
- Staleness (never helpful = low confidence)
- Temporal patterns (helpful 2 years ago = may be outdated)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:02.833061-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:28.707149-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-q23","title":"[LLM] generateObjectSafe - Schema validation with retry","description":"Function signature:
async function generateObjectSafe<T>(
  schema: z.ZodSchema<T>,
  prompt: string,
  config: Config,
  maxAttempts: number = 2
): Promise<T>

Implementation:
1. Call generateObject with schema and prompt
2. Validate result with Zod (double-check - SDK should already validate)
3. If validation fails (malformed LLM response):
   a. Modify prompt to emphasize JSON format requirements
   b. Add schema example to prompt
   c. Slightly increase temperature (e.g., 0.3 → 0.35) to avoid repeating same error
   d. Retry (up to maxAttempts)
4. Return validated result

Enhanced prompt on retry:
'[PREVIOUS ATTEMPT FAILED - OUTPUT MUST BE VALID JSON]

{original prompt}

CRITICAL: Your response MUST match this exact schema:
{JSON.stringify(schema, null, 2)}

Example valid output:
{schemaExample}'

Handles edge cases where LLM produces malformed JSON or misses required fields.

Used by: All generateObject calls in production code.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:11.328654-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:11.312851-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-q4v","title":"[Reflector] buildReflectorPrompt - Dynamic prompt construction","description":"function buildReflectorPrompt(
  diary: DiaryEntry,
  sessionContent: string,
  existingBullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  iteration: number
): string

Constructs comprehensive prompt with:
- Existing bullets summary (by category) - provides context for helpful/harmful marks
- Diary fields (accomplishments, decisions, challenges, etc.) - structured insights
- Session content (truncated if needed) - raw material for analysis
- Cass history for cross-agent context - learn from other agents
- Iteration note ("This is iteration N. Focus on insights you may have missed.")

PROMPT STRUCTURE:
1. System context (what reflection is for)
2. Existing bullets (summarizeExistingBullets)
3. Diary extraction fields
4. Cross-agent history (fetchCrossAgentHistory)
5. Session content snippet
6. Iteration guidance (getIterationNote)
7. Output schema instructions

DEPENDENCIES:
- summarizeExistingBullets()
- fetchCrossAgentHistory()
- getIterationNote()","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:52.839075-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:49.643548-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-q858","title":"[Docs] Command reference documentation","description":"Create comprehensive command reference documentation for all cass-memory commands.

For each command document:
- Command name and purpose: One-line description
- Usage syntax: cass-memory <command> [options] [args]
- Arguments: Positional args with descriptions
- Options: All flags with type, default, description
- Examples: 3-5 real-world examples
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 error, 2 validation failure, etc.
- JSON output format: Schema for --json mode

Commands to document:
- init, context, diary, reflect, validate, mark, audit, playbook, project, forget, stats, doctor, top, stale, why, similar

Target: Complete reference with copy-paste examples","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:27.309716-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:13.119397-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-qbdq","title":"[Critical] Zero-Config Quick Start Mode - Immediate Value Without Setup","description":"# Zero-Config Quick Start Mode

## The Problem
Current plan requires: init → config → reflect → wait for sessions → curate → then finally get value.
This is too many steps before users see ANY benefit. Most will abandon before step 3.

## The Solution
Make `cm context "my task"` work IMMEDIATELY, even with zero setup:

### Day 1 (No Setup Required):
```bash
# User just runs this - no init, no config
cm context "fix the auth bug"

# Output (even with empty playbook):
# Checking your recent agent sessions...
# Found 3 relevant sessions from Claude Code and Cursor
# 
# ## Relevant Past Work:
# - Session from 2 hours ago: Fixed JWT validation (similar: auth)
# - Session from yesterday: Updated user model schema
# 
# No playbook rules yet. Run 'cm reflect' to start learning from your sessions.
```

### How It Works:
1. Auto-detect ~/.cass directory (create if needed with sensible defaults)
2. Auto-find cass sessions in standard locations
3. Show value immediately from raw cass history
4. Gradually introduce playbook as it gets populated

### Config Auto-Detection:
1. Check ~/.cass-memory/config.json → use if exists
2. Check ./.cass/config.json → use if exists
3. Otherwise → use sensible defaults, no file needed

## Why This Is Critical
- Users need to see value in < 30 seconds
- If first experience is "run init, then configure, then..." they leave
- cass history search alone is valuable - don't hide it behind setup
- Playbook is a bonus, not a prerequisite

## Success Metrics
- Time to first useful output: < 5 seconds
- Required setup steps: 0
- Commands needed for basic usage: 1 (`cm context "task"`)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:05.870433-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:53:56.287174-05:00","comments":[{"id":27,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-qbdq","author":"jemanuel","text":"CRITICAL FOR USER ADOPTION - This is the #1 priority!

## Problem
Current flow requires too many steps before users see value. Users will abandon by step 5.

## Solution
Zero-config mode that provides value IMMEDIATELY using local keyword extraction + cass search (NO LLM required).

## Acceptance Criteria
- context command works without any configuration
- Works without API keys
- Returns useful results from cass history alone
- Clear messaging about "quick-start mode"
","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
